title: Tournefortia viridiflora
intro: Tournefortia viridiflora, also known as the "Tournefortia vine" or "Wild Tobacco", is a species of flowering plant in the Bignoniaceae family native to Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate conditions such as fever, coughs, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tournefortia viridiflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used to make topical creams and ointments for skin conditions and wounds.

This family comprises around 130 genera and over 2,000 species. The genus Tournefortia, to which the species belongs, is native to tropical America and is characterized by its shrub-like or tree-like growth habit. Tournefortia viridiflora is a species within this genus and is native to Mexico and Central America. It is a part of the plant kingdom, specifically the kingdom Plantae, which is a vast and diverse group of organisms.
